steps.title: Locate Rowe's criticism
steps.content: Text 2 says the old view of birdsong as male was shaped by bias: researchers often worked near temperate northern universities, where female birdsong is less common than in the tropics.

steps.title: Separate site bias from claims
steps.content: A says researchers tended to study males, but Text 2 blames study sites. B concerns why male song evolved. C compares male song purposes by region. The remaining option focuses on the chosen research locations. The answer is D.
